by_judge.judge: C M POONACHA WILL SIT IN COURT HALL NO.6 AT DHARWAD

narrative:
On July 20, 2026, the Karnataka High Court recorded an overall hearing rate of 44.5%, with judges presiding over 1,165 of the 2,620 scheduled cases. This represents a decline in conversion efficiency compared to the prior session on July 17, 2026, which posted a 49.2% hearing rate (1,037 of 2,108 cases). This dip occurred despite a substantial increase in docket volume, which expanded by over 500 scheduled cases.

Individual judge workloads and performance varied widely. Justice M. Nagaprasanna in Hall 14 managed the highest caseload, hearing 175 of 349 scheduled cases across multiple lists. Justice S Vishwajith Shetty in Hall 19 also faced high volume, hearing 92 of 273 scheduled matters (33.7%). Notable high-efficiency performances came from Justice Shivashankar Amarannavar in Hall 20, who heard 57 of 70 cases (81.4%), and Justice V Srishananda in Hall 21, who cleared 79 of 105 matters (75.2%). Conversely, scheduled lists for Justice B M Shyam Prasad (140 cases) and Justice Hanchate Sanjeevkumar (90 cases) saw no cases heard.

Analyzing procedural stages, "Orders" dominated scheduling with 723 cases, yielding a 46.7% hearing rate. "Admission" stages converted at 49.9% (219 of 439 cases), while "Final Hearing" matters lagged significantly at a 21.0% clearance rate (109 of 518 cases)—a sharp drop from the 35.0% final hearing rate observed on July 17. In terms of case types, Writ Petitions (WP) constituted the largest share with 1,100 scheduled and 440 heard (40.0%), followed by Criminal Petitions (CRL.P) at 46.3% (205 of 443) and Miscellaneous First Appeals (MFA) at 50.5% (196 of 388). Regular Second Appeals (RSA) showed the highest conversion among major types, clearing 69.8% of its 129 scheduled cases.
